event: pancreatic adenocarcinoma with metastasis to the liver
found to have x-ray findings consistent with pneumonia
receiving small boluses of fluid for hyponatremia
transferred to the intensive care unit
visually estimated left ventricular ejection fraction of 45%-50%
elevated RV systolic pressure (RVSP = 53 mm Hg)
type 2 and type 3 pulmonary arterial hypertension
ill-defined thickening in the TV leaflets
blood cultures were obtained on day 1
normal left ventricular size and function
hypermobile interatrial septum without patent foramen ovale
left atrial or left atrial appendage thrombus
2 large vegetations on the atrial aspect of MV leaflets
severe mitral regurgitation with systolic flow reversal in the pulmonary vein
imaging of the TV showed a large 1.6 × 1.1 cm vegetation
tricuspid regurgitation and RVSP 47 mm Hg
blood cultures were negative for growth on 4 repeat cultures
Polymerase chain reaction testing for Coxiella urnetiid, Legionella spp, and Brucella spp was also negative
workup for antiphospholipid syndrome was unremarkable
previously treated with the direct-acting oral anticoagulant apixaban
discontinued by their hematologist oncologist due to their severe thrombocytopenia
recommended that the patient be started on low molecular weight heparin or unfractionated heparin
computed tomography imaging of the head was completed
started on enoxaparin once platelets had recovered to above 50,000 109/L
